explicit_text: I was born and raised in the Philippines until 1992. My parents are from Quezon province, with my father from Lucena and my mother from Pagbilao. Growing up there, I attended St. John's Academy and experienced many important milestones like my first communion. Even though my dad was away in the U.S. Army, my mom and aunt took care of me while my mom worked. The Philippines is where my early childhood memories and family roots are deeply connected.

explicit_text: My dad moved to the United States when I was just one year old because he was in the U.S. Army. In 1988, my mom and I got tourist visas and visited him in California, where I experienced American holidays like Halloween and Christmas for the first time. We stayed for a limited time, but it was special to be together as a family. It took nine years before we were finally reunited permanently in the U.S. The immigration system made it difficult for us, and families still face similar challenges today.
